title: Safety-aligned subset (expert-signed, stricter threshold, disclaimered)
context: Safety/hazard/emergency/medication-related pictograms can cause harm if misunderstood — a misread 'do not drink this water' or 'emergency exit' symbol is dangerous. This subset is treated as medium risk with a privileged pipeline: stricter comprehension threshold (≥85% correct + <5% critical confusion), mandatory domain-expert sign-off, original geometry aligned to recognized concepts (AIGA/DOT PD, ISO 7010/7001/GHS concepts — no copied artwork), and an explicit 'not certified safety signage / not medical advice' disclaimer on every symbol. A safety symbol that fails its threshold is redesigned or withdrawn, never shipped.
objective: Draw, lint, expert-review, comprehension-test, and export the safety-aligned subset; each symbol meeting the stricter ≥85% correct / <5% critical-confusion threshold with domain-expert sign-off and disclaimer.
acceptanceCriteria: Each safety symbol aligns to recognized concepts (AIGA/DOT PD references, ISO 7010/7001/GHS concept meanings — no copied artwork) and is original geometry with originality attestation in provenance.

Meets the stricter comprehension bar: ≥85% correct comprehension and <5% critical confusion with the target population; any symbol failing this threshold is redesigned and retested or withdrawn — never shipped below threshold.

Each symbol has domain-expert sign-off recorded in provenance (expert name/role/date); the 'not certified safety signage / not medical advice' disclaimer is attached to every safety-aligned symbol in the metadata disclaimer field.
Safety symbols are excluded from the general batch pipeline run; they follow the safety-review SOP (writing-002) and are reviewed separately before merging.
Provenance is complete and auditable; no license or originality violations; all symbols export cleanly through the CI pipeline.
resources: open-pictograms-batch-001 (core communication vocabulary — for context on what is already covered)
ISO 7010 (safety signs — concepts only, standard not redistributed)
output: symbols/safety/ (original SVGs for the safety-aligned subset), metadata/ (JSON records with comprehensionScore, expert sign-off, and disclaimer fields), and CI-generated exports; all CC-BY-4.0 with disclaimer text. PR opened against main.
